core_mechanism: Semiclassical (eikonal/WKB) reduction of a wave problem in a self-consistent, spatially modulated complex order field to first-order transport (Riccati/Andreev-type) equations integrated along ballistic straight-line trajectories, in which sign/phase reversals of the field bind localized peaks in the local spectral density
brief_reason: The abstract solves the quasiclassical Eilenberger theory for the three-dimensional structure of a spatially varying pair potential and finds that a pi-phase shift of the field at a nodal plane or core induces sharp peak states in the local density of states, which is exactly a trajectory-based semiclassical field solution with defect-bound midgap states.
